commands.description: Initialize a library object
Use a library function
Returns the number of milliseconds passed since the Arduino board began running the current program; essential for non-blocking timing without using delay().
Configures a pin to trigger an Interrupt Service Routine (ISR) on specific signal changes (RISING, FALLING, CHANGE), allowing immediate response to external events.
Uses the EEPROM library to store and retrieve complex data structures (not just bytes) in non-volatile memory, surviving power cycles.
Sends raw binary data from a buffer to the Serial port rather than converting it to human-readable ASCII, optimizing bandwidth for high-speed data logging.
Utilizes AVR-specific sleep modes to reduce current consumption during idle periods, critical for battery-operated IoT applications.
